bead.title: agent: keep non-semantic failures from raising min power
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The worker still treats non-semantic retry classes, including repeated pre-claim system-unready warnings, as if they were the same as acceptance failures. That can raise the requested min power or profile even though a stronger model cannot fix the underlying problem.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` already defines the normative boundary: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` escalates, while dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict outcomes do not. In the worker loop, however, the retry/escalation decision is not consistently reduced to that typed table before min-power/profile selection, so system warnings and structural failures can still drift into the escalation path.

PROPOSED FIX
- Wire the retry classifier into the worker path that chooses the next requested min power or profile.
- Treat repeated pre-claim system-unready warnings as operator attention or same-tier retry, not escalation.
- Keep decomposed-parent outcomes, land coordination failures, and dirty generated DDx evidence out of the min-power escalation path.
- Allow only semantic acceptance/review failures to raise min power/profile.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review gating or land reconciliation mechanics in this child.
- Do not alter the low-level failure-class enum unless the worker path needs the typed mapping.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile.
2. Land coordination, repeated pre-claim system warnings, decomposed-parent outcomes, and dirty generated DDx evidence do not raise min power.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'RetryEscalation|Preclaim' -count=1` passes.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
